widgets_values:
# 2D Motion Settings

Use the **Zoom
** node value below to adjust ZOOM.
Positive value zoom-in, Negative value zoom-out.

Use the **X Translation and Y Translation** nodes to adjust horizontal and vertical motion speed. In pixels per frame.


Use the **Rotation** node value below to adjust Rotation.
Positive value rotate the canva to the left, Negative value rotate the canva to the right.
In degrees per frame.

# Sampler and Scheduler

You can "schedule" the sampler and noise schedulers used over the duration of the animation by enabling the "use_override" buttons on the left.

If set to TRUE the current frame will be set to whatever is used in the Value Schedule node. 

If set to FALSE then the value in the Sampler Selector and Scheduler Selector nodes will be used for the entirety of the generation sequence.

Make sure to use valid sampler and scheduler names if using the overrides. You can check the list of possible names by clicking on the "sampler_name" combo fields on the left.

"0": "tiny cute bunny, vibrant diffraction, highly detailed, intricate, ultra hd, sharp photo, crepuscular rays, in focus",
"25": "anthropomorphic clean cat, surrounded by fractals, epic angle and pose, symmetrical, 3d, depth of field",
"50": "a beautiful coconut"

# Flux 3D Deforum: Depth-Warp + Color-Coherence + Cadence + Optical Flow

A schedule-driven, **depth-aware 3D** animation loop for ComfyUI, built on the Deforum
feedback-loop architecture and adapted for **Flux** (dev fp8).

---

## Credits
- **Deforum** — original animation engine & feedback-loop design:
  [Huemin](https://huemin.art/) (creator of [Deforum](https://deforum.art/)).
- **Deforum ComfyUI nodes** — the harness (ForLoop, schedules, Set/Get bus), workflow building by
  [Akatz](https://akatz.ai): [deforum-comfy-nodes](https://github.com/deforum-art/deforum-comfy-nodes).
  This workflow is adapted from their reference workflow.
- **3D + color nodes & SDXL adaptation** —
  [Statistical-Impossibility](https://github.com/Statistical-Impossibility), via the
  [Stat-Imp nodes](https://github.com/Statistical-Impossibility/comfyui-Stat-Imp-nodes):
  **Depth Warp 3D** (exact-Deforum 6-DOF camera warp) + **Color Match LAB / Rolling Color Match (EMA)**
  (palette coherence that stops the loop's neon/"acid" drift).
- **Frame interpolation** — FILM (Frame Interpolation for Large Motion), Google Research.

---

## Models
- **Checkpoint (required):** a distilled **SDXL** model in `checkpoints/` — works with
  **Turbo / Lightning / Hyper** (e.g. *Juggernaut X Hyper*).
- **Depth (required for 3D):** a MiDaS or Depth-Anything-V2 preprocessor from
  [comfyui_controlnet_aux](https://github.com/Fannovel16/comfyui_controlnet_aux).
- **Frame interpolation (optional):** save under `frame_interpolation/` —
  [film_net_fp16.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/frame_interpolation/resolve/main/frame_interpolation/film_net_fp16.safetensors).

## Sampler settings (SDXL Turbo / Lightning / Hyper)
- **Sampler** `euler` · **Scheduler** `simple` (or `beta`) · **CFG**=1 · **Steps** ~4–8 depending on the model.
- This config runs at **CFG =1**; these distilled models do not use a negative prompt.
- Custom-model authors sometimes recommend a **DPM++ SDE** sampler; **`euler` also works well** here —
  it's faster and deterministic (less flicker).

## Inputs
- **Seed / Num Frames / Width / Height** — basics.
- **Use Upload Image?** — TRUE = start from an uploaded image; FALSE = generate frame 0 from the prompt.
- **Default Steps / Default Denoise** — used when the matching schedule group is bypassed.

## Color coherence (anti-neon)
The feedback loop drifts toward over-saturation; these hold the palette:
- **Use external palette?** — OFF = lock color to the **generated frame 0** (default); ON = lock to
  an external palette image. *(Under the hood: histogram match in LAB; the anchor is an
  exponential moving average — "EMA" — so it can drift slowly or stay frozen.)*
- **Color match strength** — how hard each frame is pulled toward the anchor.
- **Color drift (alpha)** — on the Rolling Color Match node in the **Transform Image** group: how fast
  the palette anchor follows recent frames. `0` = lock to frame 0; higher = drift with the animation.
  Needs very small values — default **0.02**.

## Cadence (speed-up)
- **Cadence (1=off)** — diffuse only every **Nth** frame; the in-betweens are warped +
  cross-dissolved, not sampled. `N=4` ≈ 4× fewer diffusion passes → much faster. `1` = off
  (every frame sampled). New content fades in over the *following* N frames — vanilla Deforum
  behavior, so motion stays smooth.
- **Optical flow (optional)** — *Compute Optical Flow* + **flow_factor** warp the in-betweens
  along estimated motion before dissolving, for smoother tweens. `None` = plain dissolve (default,
  no extra deps). DIS/Farneback need `opencv-contrib-python`; RAFT needs `torchvision`. On
  distilled SDXL keep it `None` or mild (flow_factor ~0.2); it pays off more on cleaner/heavier models.

## 3D motion
Edit the schedules in the **Transform Image** group (zoom, pan, rotate, FOV) for camera moves.
Optional **Denoise / Steps / Seed** schedule groups can be un-bypassed to vary those over time.

## Run
Configure inputs → **Queue**. Frames render in a loop and are decoded + assembled into a video at the end.

---
*The default prompt schedule is the classic Deforum / AUTOMATIC1111 demo prompt,
kept as a small tribute to the original Deforum lineage.
